libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variable, Docker service, or extra Maven dependency appears required. Generate tests against APIs present in org.apache.pekko:pekko-actor_2.13:2.0.0-M1, such as ActorSystem, Props, Actor/AbstractActor, Patterns.ask, configuration, scheduling, event stream, and serialization utilities. Always terminate ActorSystems. Do not use the removed classic TypedActor/TypedProps APIs that existed in 1.1.5.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8742 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.pekko:pekko-actor_2.13:2.0.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing 1.1.5 tests that import org.apache.pekko.actor.TypedActor, TypedActorExtension, or TypedProps will not compile against 2.0.0-M1; this is an API-change/test-update issue, not missing setup.
Pekko TestKit is commonly used in documentation for actor tests, but core behavioral coverage can be written without adding pekko-testkit.
If retaining tests for DNS internals, local Gradle verification should confirm whether the existing java.base/sun.net.dns add-exports remains necessary.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Pekko Actor 2.0.0-M1 resolves with only scala-library and com.typesafe:config as normal transitive dependencies, and meaningful actor coverage is reachable in-process with the core pekko-actor artifact.
